steps.title: Recognize the contrast word “But”
steps.content: The opening describes where the speaker physically is: “I wander among the hills of alien lands.” Then “But” signals a shift to the speaker's internal state. This is the key signal—the underlined lines function to reveal what exists within the speaker despite external location.

steps.title: Identify what the speaker feels internally
steps.content:
The underlined lines express the speaker's sense of home pulling at them—”eastern billows” and “voices of my native strands” represent the homeland calling to consciousness.

The function is conveying this internal yearning while physically abroad.

The others either assume initial travel enthusiasm that isn't stated, shift focus from homeland to relatives, or wrongly set the action as a past return.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
